final_llm_response: {"summary": "Evidence suggests that ultrasound can be used to diagnose primary frozen shoulder, with sensitivity and specificity of 90-100% and 85-95%, respectively. However, the test has limitations due to its inability to differentiate between frozen shoulder and other conditions. Further research is needed to improve diagnostic accuracy.
